---
title: "Axis reversed on tabletpc with rotated screen."
date: 2009-03-22
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by kjgillis on 2009-03-22
Moving the tablet stylus horizontally moves the mouse vertically and vice-versa. This is only a problem when the screen is rotated. I'm running ubuntu jaunty on a lenovo x61t. Here is my xorg.conf:

```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# Note that some configuration settings that could be done previously
# in this file, now are automatically configured by the server and settings
# here are ignored.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver        "wacom"
	Identifier    "stylus"
	Option        "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option        "Type"          "stylus"
	Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"           # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver        "wacom"
	Identifier    "eraser"
	Option        "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom" # USB ONLY?
	Option        "Type"          "eraser"
	Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"           # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver        "wacom"
	Identifier    "cursor"
	Option        "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom" # USB ONLY?
	Option        "Type"          "cursor"
	Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"           # Tablet PC ONLY
	#  Option        "USB"           "on"               # USB ONLY
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier    "Default Layout"
	Screen        "Default Screen"
	InputDevice   "stylus"  "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice   "eraser"  "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice   "cursor"  "SendCoreEvents" # For non-LCD tablets only
	#  InputDevice   "pad"                      # For Intuos3/CintiqV5/Graphire4/Bamboo tablets
	# InputDevice   "touch"   "SendCoreEvents" # Only a few TabletPCs support this type
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	"DontZap"	"False"
EndSection


```

Also right clicking and erasing don't work with my stylus. Thanks!

---

### Post by Favux on 2009-03-22
Hi kjgillis,

The cursor is for a Wacom hockey puck mouse on an external graphics tablet so I removed it.  I also removed it and Pad (same reason) from "ServerLayout".  I added the stylus button as a right mouse click for you.

The behavior of your stylus is consistent with your rotation method not having xsetwacom commands to change the orientation of your stylus pointer and eraser.  So if you're just using the gnome rotate applet that's why.  Please see:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=996830]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=996830")

I don't know if Tom Jaeger's wacomrotate daemon (method 3) will work for you because Jaunty changed to Xserver 1.6.  So if you try it and it does I'd love to know.

Eraser only works with certain programs and only when configured with them.  See post #8 on the above thread.

Please see attached xorg.conf.  Be sure to back yours up and compare the two.

I hope this helps.

---

### Post by kjgillis on 2009-03-22
Thanks, that fixed most of it. The wacomrotate daemon worked and right clicking works now. Pressure sensitivity works too. When I put the eraser in range of the screen the cursor jumps to the screens corner and stays there when I move the stylus. Any ideas?

---

### Post by Favux on 2009-03-22
Hi kjgillis,

Good!  Progress.

Thanks for letting me know that Tom's wacomrotate daemon works in Jaunty.

The behavior of eraser you are describing is usually consistent with X not getting any input.  I went over the xorg.conf I uploaded and everything looks OK.  Maybe you should post your current xorg.conf.  The xorg.conf you posted is consistent with a built in serial tablet.  Do you know for sure that the X61t has an inbuilt serial wacom tablet and not a usb one?

I think of the eraser as a special button on the stylus.  Your eraser sits on the back of the stylus and retracts into the barrel of the stylus when depressed, correct?

Do you know which version of linuxwacom and wacom tools you have installed?  Try Synaptics to check.  I think they were talking 0.8.2 for Jaunty.

---

### Post by kjgillis on 2009-03-23
It looks like wacom-tools is 0.8.1.6. Searching for linuxwacom in synaptic came up with xserver-xorg-input-wacom which is also 0.8.1.6. I checked online and my tablet should be a serial device. lsusb didn't show a tablet. 

You description of the eraser is correct. Also pressing the eraser button works, just not moving the cursor with the eraser. The cursor is stuck in the lower corner so the only thing I can click with the eraser is the trash icon.

---

### Post by Favux on 2009-03-23
Hi kjgillis,

Boy, that is not such great news.  I was hoping for at least 0.8.2.  The N-trig digitizer folks and some others will not be happy.

When you flip the stylus over does the pointer start tracking the stylus tip?

Either way this sounds like a glitch.  You could try going into Synaptic and telling it to reinstall linuxwacom and wacom-tools.

By the way did you calibrate with "wacomcpl"?  If so check .xinitrc (a hidden file) in your home/usrename directory and see if it has eraser settings.

As a last resort you could try installing newer drivers on this thread:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1038949]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1038949")

But I do **not** recommend this, especially with alpha Jaunty and things in flux.

Edit:  I just thought of something.  0.8.1-6 is actually on LWP's "developement" branch.  Although it's been out for a few months it seems to now be working with Xserver 1.6 (which Jaunty has).  So it might actually be a more "updated" version than I realize.  I just may not understand their versioning nomenclature well enough.

---

### Post by kjgillis on 2009-03-23
When the stylus is facing the screen everything tracks fine.

I tried calibrating with wacomcpl and it made the eraser work. I logged out to see if it would keep working but it didn't. I don't seem to have an .initrc. I checked with hidden files showing. Is wacomcpl supposed to modify that file so it fixes the calibration each time you login or something?

---

### Post by Favux on 2009-03-23
Hi kjgillis,

Outstanding!  Then we should be there.

> Is wacomcpl supposed to modify that file so it fixes the calibration each time you login or something? 
Yes.  The link I gave above for last resort-go to section 3.  It will explain how to make the .xinitrc settings permanent.

### Post by kjgillis on 2009-03-23
I fixed that last problem with the script I found here:[https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/wacom-tools/+bug/295292/comments/15](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/wacom-tools/+bug/295292/comments/15)

---

### Post by Favux on 2009-03-23
Hi kjgillis,

Sweet.  And the point is you can use the xsetwacom command in a terminal to change behavior on the "fly".  And obviously you can set them up in a shell script.  You could tie the script to a launcher if you want a certain configuration for a specific program.

Belatedly I remember that Jaunty is suppose to have two useful new tools (gui's).  Could you look around and see if there is something called "Graphical Configuration Tool for Wacom Tablets & TabletPCs" and "Xorg.conf Options Editor"?  I would greatly appreciate it.

---

### Post by kjgillis on 2009-03-25
